title: XMiete Core Schema
description: A modular standard for digital rental deposits across European jurisdictions.

jurisdiction.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code governing this deposit. Drives deposit cap, return deadline, interest obligation, and escrow requirements.

jurisdiction_sub.description: ISO 3166-2 subdivision code for jurisdictions with regional rules (e.g. 'ES-AN' for Andalucía, 'GB-SCT' for Scotland). Required for Spain (regional fianza registration) and UK (Scotland vs. England caps).

tax_id.description: National tax identifier. Format varies by jurisdiction: DE 11-digit Steuer-ID, FR 15-digit NIR, IT 16-char Codice Fiscale, ES 9-char NIF/NIE, NL 9-digit BSN, UK NI number.

tax_id_type.description: Type of tax identifier supplied in tax_id.

name.description: Name of landlord or property management

lease.description: Lease financial parameters used for deposit cap validation and return deadline calculation.

monthly_cold_rent.description: Monthly base rent excluding utilities (Kaltmiete / loyer hors charges / rent excluding service charge). Reference figure for cap validation in DE, AT, CH.

monthly_warm_rent.description: Monthly rent including utilities. Reference figure for jurisdictions that cap against total rent (e.g. UK, IE, NL).

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency of the rent. Non-EUR: GBP (UK), CHF (CH), NOK (NO), SEK (SE), DKK (DK), PLN (PL), CZK (CZ), HUF (HU).

lease_end.description: Contractual end date. Absent for open-ended leases.

type.description: CASH_EQUIVALENT: tenant pays cash into a segregated account or government-approved scheme. BANK_GUARANTEE: bank issues a guarantee in lieu of cash — tenant retains liquidity. INSURANCE: deposit insurance product (annual premium paid by tenant; insurer covers landlord claims). SURETY: personal guarantee by a third party (parent, employer, or other individual); guarantor contractually promises to cover tenant obligations. Requires at least one guarantor[] entry.

return_deadline.description: Jurisdiction-specific latest date by which the deposit must be returned or deductions communicated. Set from handover_date + jurisdiction rule (e.g. 10 days for LV, 14 days for NL clean, 30 days for FR/ES/CZ, ~6 months practice for DE).

actor.description: Entity that triggered the transition (e.g., TENANT, BANK, LANDLORD)

signature.description: JWS (JSON Web Signature) for non-repudiation of critical transitions.

installment_plan.description: Installment schedule when the tenant pays in tranches. DE: up to 3 instalments (BGB § 551 Abs. 2); other jurisdictions: no statutory limit but contractually agreed.

total_installments.description: Number of equal installments. In DE, BGB § 551 Abs. 2 limits this to 3; other jurisdictions have no statutory cap.

due_date.description: Contractual due date for this tranche.

paid_date.description: Actual payment date — absent if not yet paid. Used as the interest accrual start date for this tranche.

amount.description: Actual amount paid for this tranche.

pledge.description: Deposit pledge data — statutory basis varies by jurisdiction.

statutory_basis.description: Jurisdiction-specific statutory basis for the pledge (e.g. 'BGB § 551' for DE, 'Art. 257e CO' for CH, 'LAU Art. 36' for ES, 'Housing Act 2004' for GB).

is_confirmed_by_bank.description: Omit when unknown; false means explicitly not confirmed.

trusteeship.description: Deposit account configuration. Structure and legal requirements vary significantly by jurisdiction.

account_type.description: TREUHANDKONTO: DE/AT standard segregated landlord-held trust account. ANDERKONTO: DE restricted to licensed professionals (lawyers, notaries). POOLED_TREUHAND: DE multiple deposits in one designated account with per-tenant sub-accounting. MIETKAUTIONSKONTO: CH/NO blocked account in tenant's name — landlord has zero access without tenant consent or court order (Art. 257e CO; Husleieloven §3-5). CUSTODIAL_SCHEME: UK government-approved scheme holds funds directly (TDS, DPS, SafeDeposits Scotland). INSURED_SCHEME: UK landlord holds funds, insured via government-approved scheme (MyDeposits, TDS insured). EDEPO: BE mandatory blocked escrow via Belgian e-DEPO system — cannot be paid to landlord directly. FIANZA_REGIONAL: ES legal fianza (1 month) held by regional authority (INCASOL, IVIMA, etc.). STATE_GUARANTEE: LU state-backed guarantee for low-income tenants. DEPOSIT_INSURANCE: CH/DE alternative to cash deposit — annual premium product (SwissCaution, goCaution, getmomo). LANDLORD_HELD: FR/NL/IT/most CEE — landlord holds funds without statutory escrow requirement.

permitted_payment_flows.description: Explicitly permitted payment routes for initial funding. Belgium (e-DEPO) allows only ['TENANT_TO_TRUSTEE']; Germany and UK-Insured allow ['TENANT_TO_LANDLORD', 'LANDLORD_TO_TRUSTEE'].

trustee_entity.description: Full legal name of the entity holding or guaranteeing the funds.

trust_account_iban.description: IBAN of the designated trust or blocked account.

insolvency_protection_confirmed.description: Bank-confirmed insolvency-proof separation. Primarily relevant for DE (BGB § 551 Abs. 3), AT, and CH. Omit when unknown; false means explicitly not confirmed.

insolvency_protection_confirmed_date.description: ISO 8601 date on which insolvency-proof separation was confirmed.

statutory_basis.description: Jurisdiction-specific statutory basis for the account arrangement (e.g. 'BGB § 551 Abs. 3' for DE, 'Art. 257e CO' for CH, 'Housing Act 2004 s.212' for GB, 'Woninghuurwet Art. 10' for BE).

interest_required.description: Whether the jurisdiction legally requires interest to be paid to the tenant on the deposit. True for DE, AT, CZ, HU, IT; false for FR, NL, ES, IE, and most CEE.

interest_rate.description: Current annual interest rate as a decimal (e.g. 0.015 for 1.5%). Required when interest_required is true.

interest_rate_history.description: [Tenant transparency layer] Full history of rate changes, enabling tenants to independently verify accrued interest over the tenancy duration.

effective_to.description: Absent if this is the current rate.

accrued_interest.description: [Tenant transparency layer] Bank-calculated interest accrued to date. Informational snapshot — bank account statement governs legally.

interest_calculated_at.description: [Tenant transparency layer] Timestamp when accrued_interest and total_balance were last calculated.

total_balance.description: [Tenant transparency layer] Principal + accrued_interest at interest_calculated_at.

protection_scheme.description: Government-approved deposit protection scheme registration. Required where scheme registration is mandatory (GB, BE) or carries statutory consequences (IE pending 2025 Bill). Omit in jurisdictions without a scheme.

scheme_name.description: Approved scheme identifier. TDS/DPS/MYDEPOSITS: England & Wales. SAFEDEPOSITS_SCOTLAND/LPS_SCOTLAND/MYDEPOSITS_SCOTLAND: Scotland (custodial only). TDSNORTHERNIRELAND: Northern Ireland. EDEPO_BE: Belgium. RTB_IE: Ireland (if Protection of Tenants' Deposits Bill 2025 is enacted). OTHER: use with scheme_name_other.

scheme_name_other.description: Free-text scheme name when scheme_name is OTHER.

registration_number.description: Scheme-issued registration or certificate reference. Must be provided to the tenant within the statutory window (30 days in GB).

registration_date.description: Date the deposit was registered with the scheme.

registration_deadline.description: Statutory deadline for registration (e.g. deposit receipt + 30 days for GB, deposit receipt + 28 days for NI).

protection_type.description: CUSTODIAL: scheme holds the funds. INSURED: landlord holds funds; scheme insures against non-return. Scotland allows custodial only.

statutory_basis.description: Statutory basis for the registration requirement (e.g. 'Housing Act 2004 s.213' for GB, 'Woninghuurwet Art. 10' for BE).

provider.description: Information about the bank, insurer, or fintech platform providing the deposit.

executing_entity.description: The legal entity holding the funds or issuing the guarantee.

brand_name.description: The brand or platform name facing the user.

transport.description: Transport protocol configuration for bank-system communication. Defaults to REST. EBICS is used primarily in DE, AT, CH, FR — not standard in GB, Nordics, or CEE.

utility_reservation.description: Portion of the deposit held back while annual utility reconciliation is pending. Primarily applicable in DE (BGB § 556 Abs. 3) and AT where Betriebskosten are billed annually. Not applicable in most FR, GB, NL, and CEE tenancies where utility billing is handled separately from the deposit.

released_amount.description: Amount immediately returned to the tenant at tenancy end.

reserved_amount.description: Amount held back pending utility reconciliation.

monthly_advance.description: Monthly utility advance payment — informational basis for the reservation size.

billing_period_end.description: End of the utility billing period the reservation covers.

resolution_deadline.description: Latest date for the utility statement. In DE: billing_period_end + 12 months (BGB § 556 Abs. 3). After this date the reservation must be released regardless.

settlement.description: Structured settlement negotiation at tenancy end. Either party proposes an itemized split; the other accepts or counter-proposes.

handover_protocol_ref.description: Reference to the signed handover / check-out report (Übergabeprotokoll, état des lieux, check-out inventory).

category.description: COSMETIC_REPAIRS: jurisdiction-specific enforceability varies — frequently unenforceable in DE (BGH case law on Schönheitsreparaturen), often valid in GB and FR if evidenced.

evidence_refs.description: URLs or document IDs: photos, invoices, contractor quotes, rent ledger.

response_deadline.description: Response window for the counter-party. 14 days mirrors UK DPS/TDS and NL Huurcommissie practice; adjust per jurisdiction.

escalation_type.description: PLATFORM_MEDIATION: platform's own mediation service. SPECIALIST_TRIBUNAL: government-mandated ADR body (GB: TDS/DPS/MyDeposits ADR; NL: Huurcommissie; NO: Husleietvistutvalget; IE: RTB; DK: Huslejenævn; SE: Hyresnämnden; FI: Consumer Disputes Board). CIVIL_COURT: general court jurisdiction (FR, ES, IT, PT, GR, most CEE). REGIONAL_AUTHORITY: dispute handled by the regional body holding the deposit (ES fianza disputes).

specialist_tribunal_id.description: Identifies the specific ADR body when escalation_type is SPECIALIST_TRIBUNAL.

external_authority.description: Name of the court or authority once escalated.

external_reference.description: Court or authority case number once escalated.

agreed_tenant_refund.description: Set on CLOSED — final amount returned to tenant.

agreed_landlord_retention.description: Set on CLOSED — final amount retained by landlord.

guarantor.description: Personal surety arrangements — one entry per guarantor. Present when deposit.type is SURETY (primary security is a personal guarantee, no cash deposit) or alongside a cash deposit when the landlord requires additional guarantor security (common in GB student lettings). Most jurisdictions use a single guarantor; multiple entries are valid (e.g. FR co-cautions, GB joint guarantors).

name.description: Full legal name of the guarantor.

relationship.description: Guarantor's relationship to the tenant. PARENT: parental guarantee (DE Elternbürgschaft, FR caution parentale, GB common for student lettings). EMPLOYER: employer acts as guarantor. THIRD_PARTY: any other private individual or company. STATE_BODY: public body guarantee (LU state guarantee for low-income tenants; BE CPAS/OCMW social guarantee).

guarantee_type.description: Legal form of the guarantee. SELBSTSCHULDNERISCH: DE/AT — solidary guarantee (§ 773 BGB Abs. 1 Nr. 1 waiver); landlord can pursue guarantor directly without first exhausting remedies against tenant — the variant most landlords require. AUSFALLBUERGSCHAFT: DE/AT — deficiency/subsidiary guarantee; landlord must first prove the tenant is unable to pay before claiming against the guarantor. CAUTION_SOLIDAIRE: FR — solidary surety (Art. 2298 Code civil); same practical effect as SELBSTSCHULDNERISCH. CAUTION_SIMPLE: FR — simple surety; guarantor can invoke the beneficium excussionis, requiring the landlord to pursue tenant assets first. STANDARD: GB/IE/NL/Nordics and all other jurisdictions — general contractual guarantor; specific liability rules are governed by the guarantor agreement itself.

statutory_basis.description: Jurisdiction-specific statutory basis for the guarantee (e.g. 'BGB § 765' for DE, 'Art. 2288 Code civil' for FR, 'CONTRACT' for GB/IE where guarantor agreements are purely contractual with no specific tenancy statute).

scope.description: What the guarantee covers. RENT_ARREARS: unpaid rent. DAMAGE: physical damage beyond normal wear and tear. CLEANING: end-of-tenancy cleaning costs. LEGAL_COSTS: landlord's recovery costs. ALL: full liability for all tenant obligations under the lease — use ['ALL'] rather than listing each category individually.

uncapped.description: If true, no monetary cap applies — guarantor is liable for the full extent of tenant obligations. Common in GB student lettings. When true, omit cap_amount.

cap_amount.description: Maximum monetary liability of the guarantor. In DE, courts often apply the BGB § 551 3-month cold-rent cap by analogy when a Bürgschaft substitutes for a Mietkaution. Omit when uncapped is true.

validity_start.description: Date from which the guarantee is effective. Typically the lease start date.

validity_end.description: Contractual expiry of the guarantee. Absent if the guarantee runs for the full tenancy duration or is open-ended.

validity_follows_tenancy.description: If true, the guarantee is explicitly bound to the full tenancy including any renewals or extensions. In GB, guarantors must explicitly agree to be bound by lease renewals — this field records that agreement.

executed_as_deed.description: GB/IE only: whether the guarantor agreement was executed as a deed (signed, witnessed, and delivered). Recommended in GB where the tenancy agreement itself is a deed and for guarantees intended to survive lease renewals.

fr_visale_held.description: FR only: set to true if the landlord also holds a Garantie Visale for this tenancy. If true and fr_visale_student_exception is not true, requiring a personal guarantor violates ALUR Art. 22-1 — a landlord holding Garantie Visale may not also demand a caution personnelle, except for student tenants.

fr_visale_student_exception.description: FR only: set to true if the tenant is a student. Students are exempt from the ALUR Art. 22-1 prohibition, permitting the landlord to require a personal guarantor even when holding a Garantie Visale.

guarantor_category.description: PERSONAL: private individual guarantor. Form consequences by jurisdiction: DE — § 766 BGB S. 2 explicitly excludes electronic form; wet-ink only, QES does not help. CH — OR Art. 493 requires written form + handwritten signature for amounts > CHF 2,000; a QES from a recognised trust service provider satisfies this (unlike DE), but simple electronic signatures do not. AT — Schriftform required (KSchG consumer protection), but QES fully substitutes under the Austrian Signaturgesetz. FR — Art. 2297 CCiv (post-2021 reform) requires a specific written mention for consumer guarantors; QES accepted. GB — Statute of Frauds 1677 requires writing + signature; electronic writing and qualified e-signatures accepted under ECA 2000. COMMERCIAL: company or registered Kaufmann/trader — HGB § 350 (DE), OR Art. 493 Abs. 2 (CH), and equivalents elsewhere suspend all form requirements; guarantee may be concluded electronically in any XMiete jurisdiction.

signature_formality.description: How the guarantee declaration was executed. WET_INK: handwritten signature on a physical document — the only option for PERSONAL guarantors in DE/AT under § 766 BGB S. 2. QES: qualified electronic signature (eIDAS Art. 3(12)); valid for COMMERCIAL guarantors (HGB § 350) and in jurisdictions that do not exclude electronic form (GB, FR, NL, BE, NO). DEED: executed as a deed — GB/IE only; requires signing, witnessing, and delivery; use alongside executed_as_deed: true. ELECTRONIC: simple or advanced electronic signature; valid only where statute permits (non-DE/AT personal guarantors; commercial guarantors everywhere). Implementers MUST validate that PERSONAL guarantors in DE/AT use WET_INK — any other value is legally void under current German law.

document_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the canonical guarantee document (scanned signed PDF for WET_INK; signed payload for QES/DEED). Provides a tamper-evident link between this schema record and the legal instrument. Recommended for all guarantors; required for WET_INK to establish that the bank has received and holds the signed original.

signed_at.description: Timestamp of guarantor signature on the guarantee agreement.

document_ref.description: Reference to the signed guarantor agreement (file ID, URL, or document management system identifier).

allOf.description: DE personal guarantors: § 766 BGB S. 2 explicitly excludes electronic form — uniquely among XMiete jurisdictions, even a QES does not satisfy the formality requirement. PERSONAL guarantors in Germany must use WET_INK. COMMERCIAL guarantors (HGB § 350) are exempt from all form requirements and may use any signature_formality. Note: Austria (AT) does NOT share this prohibition — Austrian QES satisfies Schriftform under the Signaturgesetz.

CH personal guarantors: OR Art. 493 requires written form with the guarantor's handwritten signature for private individuals guaranteeing amounts over CHF 2,000. Unlike § 766 BGB S. 2 (DE), Swiss law does NOT explicitly exclude electronic form — a QES issued by a recognised Swiss or EU trust service provider satisfies the handwritten-signature requirement under ZertES (SR 943.03). Simple or advanced electronic signatures (ELECTRONIC) do not. EUDI Wallet QES is accepted in Switzerland only where the wallet provider is listed on the EU Trusted List and cross-recognised under the CH-EU mutual recognition framework.

wallet_metadata.description: eIDAS 2.0 / EUDI-Wallet credential metadata for tenant identity verification.

issuer.description: Human-readable name of the credential issuer.

issuer_id.description: URI or DID identifying the credential issuer.

wallet_provider.description: Identifier of the EUDI Wallet provider app.

credential_type.description: EUDI credential type: PID (Person Identification Data), EAA/QEAA (Electronic Attestation of Attributes), MDL (mobile Driving License).

credential_format.description: Credential encoding format per OpenID4VCI / EUDI ARF.

assurance_level.description: eIDAS Level of Assurance per Article 8 of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 as amended by eIDAS 2.0.

issuing_country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the credential issuer.

deposit_pledge_attestation.description: QEAA credential schema for DepositPledgeAttestation — issued by a bank into the tenant's EUDI Wallet after deposit pledge. Format: SD-JWT (vc+sd-jwt). Governed by OpenID4VCI Pre-Authorized Code Flow.

deposit_amount.description: [Selectively Disclosed] Amount of the pledged deposit.

pledged_until.description: [Selectively Disclosed] Date until which the pledge is in effect.

statutory_basis.description: Jurisdiction-specific statutory basis for the pledge (e.g. 'BGB § 551' for DE, 'Art. 257e CO' for CH, 'Housing Act 2004' for GB, 'LAU Art. 36' for ES). Always revealed — not selectively disclosed.

issuing_bank.description: Full legal name of the bank issuing the credential.

issuing_bank_id.description: URI or DID identifying the issuing bank.

property_address.description: [Selectively Disclosed] Human-readable address of the rental property.

ebics_metadata.description: EBICS connection parameters. Required when transport.protocol is EBICS.

ebics_version.description: Use 3.0 for BTF-based order routing; 2.4/2.5 for legacy order types.

order_type.description: EBICS 2.x three-letter order type. Not used with EBICS 3.0; use btf instead.

issuance_session.description: Tracks the state of an active OID4VCI issuance session for a deposit.

credential_offer_url.description: openid-credential-offer:// deep-link / QR payload for the tenant's wallet.
